commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(#36): cli typecheck and format failures after dag spec v2 migration (#37)

* fix(cli): update triageRecipe.nodes to definition.states after DAG spec v2 migration, fix formatting

- Replace `triageRecipe.nodes.length` with `Object.keys(triageRecipe.definition.states).length`
  in packages/cli/src/main.ts:103 — the Recipe type no longer has a `nodes` array after the
  DAG spec v2 migration; states live under `recipe.definition.states`.
- Run prettier --write on 7 files that were committed without formatting:
  runner-recipe.ts, types.ts, pagerduty.ts, prometheus.ts, their tests, studio/index.html.
  Fixes CI typecheck and format check failures on main and all dependabot branches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(action): update recipe mock shape to definition.states after DAG spec v2 migration

* fix(cli): update recipe mock shape to DAG spec v2 after engine migration

triageRecipe and implementRecipe now use `{ definition: { states: {} }, implementations: {} }`
instead of the old `{ nodes: [], edges: [] }` shape. The stale mock caused TypeError
in `main.ts:103` when accessing `.definition.states` during spinner setup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add file output to createLogger for persisting run logs (#120)

* feat: add file output to createLogger for persisting run logs

createLogger now accepts an optional logFilePath that writes log entries
to disk via appendFileSync. The run and watch commands pass
~/.aidev/runs/{runId}/run.log so execution logs are automatically saved.

* fix: Two issues: (1) appendFileSync in logger.ts had no error handling, so disk/permission errors would crash the application. (2) In cli.ts, the resume log at L189 was written before logger recreation with file output, so it was never persisted to the log file.

* fix: address logging review feedback (PR #120)

- Add setLogFile() to enable file logging after logger creation,
  fixing early log loss in run command (#1)
- Add file logging to watch command's main logger (#2)
- Replace appendFileSync with createWriteStream for async I/O (#3)
- Emit single stderr warning on file write errors instead of
  silently swallowing (#4)
- Remove backward-compat LogLevel | CreateLoggerOptions union type (#5)
- Add tmpdir cleanup in tests with afterAll (#6)
- Consolidate mkdir to cli commands, remove from persistence.save (#7)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address round 2 review feedback (PR #120)

- Remove duplicate resume log before setLogFile (#1)
- Restore mkdir in persistence.save() for safety (#2)
- Add logger.flush() before process.exit() to prevent log loss (#3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rework Google login stealth: use webFrame + Chromium feature flags

Remove separate login popup window and session/header manipulation.
Use webFrame.executeJavaScript() in preload to override
navigator.userAgentData in the page's main world before page scripts
run. Disable UserAgentClientHint/NavigatorUAData/ClientHintsBrands
at the Chromium engine level. Add CDP stealth utility for future use.

Add server-side auth protection via @supabase/ssr middleware

- Switch browser Supabase client to createBrowserClient (@supabase/ssr)
  so auth tokens are stored in cookies (readable by middleware)
- Create supabase-middleware.ts with createServerClient for middleware
- Rewrite middleware.ts to validate sessions server-side:
  - /dashboard/*, /owner/*, /seeker/*, /account/* require auth
  - Unauthenticated users redirected to /signin?redirect=<path>
  - Admin routes still handled by client-side AdminGate

causing React's getSnapshot cache violation. Fixed by selecting only
primitives (lastUpdatedDay, economyHealth) and computing modifiers
inside useMemo with getCityGameplayModifiers(getState()).

causing scoreboard_entries FK violation. Now falls back to querying the
existing week id.

commit.message:
Track users with consistently low market price confidence

Lower server MIN_CONFIDENCE to 0.60 to accept low-confidence submissions
for quality tracking. Add detectLowConfidenceUsers() that flags users
where ≥50% of submissions (min 20, 7-day window) are below 0.85.
Superseded submissions are naturally excluded by the ON CONFLICT upsert.
Display mp_low_confidence alerts in admin ingestion panel.

fix: 案件情報シートの注意書き行が2件目の案件と誤判定される問題を修正

isSampleRowで「【注意】...」等の角括弧始まりの行をスキップ対象に追加。
テンプレート4行目の注意書きが有効な案件行として検出されていた。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Optimus now detects late-PO situations (was showing ALL CLEAR incorrectly)

Root cause: optimusRules.js filtered shortages with `!s.po_pending`, silently
dropping all LATE_PO items (PO exists but arrives after critical date). These
have action_needed=true but po_pending=true, so Optimus said ALL CLEAR while
PA and KPI bar correctly showed 25+ CATASTROPHE items.

Fix:
- Change actionable filter from `!s.po_pending` to `s.action_needed` — the
  semantically correct field that already accounts for late/insufficient/oversized POs
- Add Case 0 for LATE_PO items: shows station name, PO number, delivery date,
  days until deficit, and suggests transfer or urgent supplemental order
- Sort case0 alongside other cases by days_until_critical_level
- Footer "serious" mode now triggers when case0+case1 > 3 (not just case1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add milk carton missing member feature (!haveyouseenthem, !missing)

Generates milk carton style "missing person" PNG images using gg library
with embedded Go fonts. Tracks inactive members via daily_activity, with
configurable thresholds (MISSING_THRESHOLD_DAYS, MISSING_MAX_DAYS,
MISSING_MIN_MESSAGES, MISSING_EXCLUDE_USERS). Rate limited to 1 carton
per room per day. Derives flavor text characteristics from user stats
and sentiment data. Avatar fetching with 3-tier fallback.

  budget progress, and 30-day history

commit.message:
feat(mongodb): replace collectionName with prefix for collection naming

The third constructor parameter changes from a direct collection name to a
prefix applied to both collections:
- {prefix}_events (default: app_events)
- {prefix}__event_counters (default: app__event_counters)

The double-underscore on the counters collection signals it is an internal
implementation detail not intended for direct use.

BREAKING CHANGE: existing deployments must rename collections; see CHANGELOG.
